children.name: Torgos
children.description: Torgos — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Torgos tracheliotos
children.description: The lappet-faced vulture or  Nubian vulture is an Old World vulture belonging to the bird order Accipitriformes, which also includes eagles, kites, buzzards and hawks. It is the only extant member of the genus Torgos. It is not closely related to the superficially similar New World vultures, and does not share the good sense of smell of some members of that family of birds.

children.name: Torgos tracheliotus
children.description: The lappet-faced vulture or  Nubian vulture is an Old World vulture belonging to the bird order Accipitriformes, which also includes eagles, kites, buzzards and hawks. It is the only extant member of the genus Torgos. It is not closely related to the superficially similar New World vultures, and does not share the good sense of smell of some members of that family of birds.

children.name: Chelictinia
children.description: Chelictinia — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Chelictinia riocourii
children.description: The scissor-tailed kite, also known commonly as the African swallow-tailed kite and the fork-tailed kite, is a bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It is the only species placed in the genus Chelictinia. It is widespread in the northern tropics of Africa.

children.name: Elanus
children.description: Elanus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Elanus axillaris
children.description: The black-shouldered kite, also known as the Australian black-shouldered kite, is a small raptor found in open habitats throughout Australia. It resembles similar species found in Africa, Eurasia and North America, including the black-winged kite, a species that has in the past also been called "black-shouldered kite". Measuring around 35 cm (14 in) in length, with a wingspan of 80–100 cm (31–39 in), the adult black-shouldered kite has predominantly grey-white plumage and prominent black markings above its red eyes.

children.name: Elanus caeruleus
children.description: The black-winged kite, also known as the black-shouldered kite, is a small diurnal bird of prey in the family Accipitridae best known for its habit of hovering over open grasslands in the manner of the much smaller kestrels. This Palearctic and Afrotropical species was sometimes combined with the Australian black-shouldered kite and the white-tailed kite of North and South America which together form a superspecies. This kite is distinctive, with long wings; white, grey and black plumage; and owl-like forward-facing eyes with red irises.

children.name: Elanus caesius
children.description: Elanus caesius — a species in the genus Elanus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Elanus leucurus
children.description: The white-tailed kite is a small raptor found in western North America and parts of South America. It replaces the related Old World black-winged kite in its native range.

children.name: Elanus scriptus
children.description: The letter-winged kite is a small, rare and irruptive bird of prey that is found only in Australia. Measuring around 35 cm (14 in) in length with a wingspan of 84–100 cm (33–39 in), the adult letter-winged kite has predominantly pale grey and white plumage and prominent black rings around its red eyes. Its name derives from its highly distinctive black underwing pattern of a shallow 'M' or 'W' shape, visible when in flight.

children.name: Harpyhaliaetus
children.description: Harpyhaliaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Harpyhaliaetus coronatus
children.description: The Chaco eagle or crowned solitary eagle, is an endangered bird of prey from eastern and central South America. Typically it is known simply as the crowned eagle, which leads to potential confusion with the African Stephanoaetus coronatus. Due to its rarity, not much is known about its biology or population.

children.name: Harpyhaliaetus solitarius
children.description: The solitary eagle or montane solitary eagle is a large Neotropical eagle. It is also known as the black solitary eagle.

children.name: Terathopius
children.description: Terathopius — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Terathopius ecaudatus
children.description: The bateleur, also known as the bateleur eagle, is a medium-sized eagle in the family Accipitridae. It is often considered a relative of the snake eagles and, like them, it is classified within the subfamily Circaetinae. It is the only member of the genus Terathopius and may be the origin of the "Zimbabwe Bird", the national emblem of Zimbabwe.

children.name: Macheiramphus
children.description: Macheiramphus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Macheiramphus alcinus
children.description: The bat hawk is a raptor found in sub-Saharan Africa and south Asia to New Guinea. It is named for its diet, which consists mainly of bats. It requires open space in which to hunt, but will live anywhere from dense rainforest to semi-arid veld.

children.name: Pithecophaga
children.description: Pithecophaga — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Pithecophaga jefferyi
children.description: The Philippine eagle, also known as the monkey-eating eagle or great Philippine eagle, is a critically endangered species of eagle of the family Accipitridae which is endemic to forests in the Philippines. It has brown and white-colored plumage, a shaggy crest, and generally measures 86 to 102 cm in length and weighs 4.04 to 8.0 kg.

children.name: Gyps
children.description: Gyps — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Gyps africanus
children.description: The white-backed vulture is an Old World vulture in the family Accipitridae, which also includes eagles, kites, buzzards and hawks. It is the most common vulture species in the continent of Africa.

children.name: Gyps bengalensis
children.description: The white-rumped vulture is an Old World vulture native to South and Southeast Asia. It has been listed as Critically Endangered on the IUCN Red List since 2000, as the population severely declined. White-rumped vultures are threatened by diclofenac poisoning, which kills them by causing kidney failure.

children.name: Gyps coprotheres
children.description: The Cape vulture, also known as Cape griffon and Kolbe's vulture, is an Old World vulture in the family Accipitridae. It is endemic to southern Africa, and lives mainly in South Africa, Lesotho, Botswana, and in some parts of northern Namibia. It nests on cliffs and lays one egg per year.

children.name: Gyps fulvus
children.description: The Eurasian griffon vulture is a large Old World vulture in the bird of prey family Accipitridae. It is also known as the Eurasian griffon or griffon vulture, although the latter term is sometimes used for the genus Gyps as a whole.

children.name: Gyps himalayensis
children.description: The Himalayan vulture or Himalayan griffon vulture is an Old World vulture native to the Himalayas and foothills in North and Northeastern India, as well as the adjacent Tibetan Plateau. After the cinereous vulture, it is the second-largest Old World vulture species, and among the world's largest true raptors. It is listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List.

children.name: Gyps indicus
children.description: The Indian vulture or long-billed vulture is a bird of prey native to the Indian subcontinent. It is an Old World vulture belonging to the family of Accipitridae. It is a medium-sized vulture with a small, semi-bald head with little feathers, long beak, and wide dark colored wings.

children.name: Gyps melitensis
children.description: Gyps melitensis — a species in the genus Gyps, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Gyps vulgaris
children.description: Gyps vulgaris — a species in the genus Gyps, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Stephanoaetus
children.description: Stephanoaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Gypohierax
children.description: Gypohierax — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Leucopternis
children.description: Leucopternis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Leucopternis semiplumbeus
children.description: The semiplumbeous hawk is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It is found in Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Honduras, and Panama. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.

children.name: Trigonoceps
children.description: Trigonoceps — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Parabuteo
children.description: Parabuteo — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Rostrhamus
children.description: Rostrhamus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Pernis
children.description: Pernis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Harpyopsis
children.description: Harpyopsis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Polemaetus
children.description: Polemaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Spilornis
children.description: Spilornis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Geranospiza
children.description: Geranospiza — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Haliastur
children.description: Haliastur — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Kaupifalco
children.description: Kaupifalco — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Haliaeetus
children.description: Haliaeetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Haliaeetus albicilla
children.description: The white-tailed eagle, sometimes known as the "sea eagle", is a large bird of prey, widely distributed across temperate Eurasia. Like all eagles, it is a member of the family Accipitridae which also includes other diurnal raptors such as hawks, kites, and harriers. One of up to eleven members in the genus Haliaeetus, which are commonly called sea eagles, it is also referred to as the white-tailed sea-eagle.

children.name: Haliaeetus nisus
children.description: Haliaeetus nisus — a species in the genus Haliaeetus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Haliaeetus piscator
children.description: Haliaeetus piscator — a species in the genus Haliaeetus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Erythrotriorchis
children.description: Erythrotriorchis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Elanoides
children.description: Elanoides — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Henicopernis
children.description: Henicopernis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Eutriorchis
children.description: Eutriorchis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Hamirostra
children.description: Hamirostra — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Lophaetus
children.description: Lophaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Butastur
children.description: Butastur — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Butastur liventer
children.description: The rufous-winged buzzard is an Asian bird of prey. It is a resident breeder of Indochina, Java and Sulawesi. It is a species of deciduous forest and second growth up to 800 m.

children.name: Circus
children.description: Circus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Circus aeruginosus
children.description: The western marsh harrier is a large harrier, a bird of prey from temperate and subtropical western Eurasia and adjacent Africa. It is also known as the Eurasian marsh harrier. Formerly, a number of relatives were included in C.

children.name: Circus macrourus x cyaneus
children.description: Circus macrourus x cyaneus — a species in the genus Circus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Circus macrourus x pygargus
children.description: Circus macrourus x pygargus — a species in the genus Circus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Circus pygargus
children.description: Montagu's harrier is a species of harrier, a bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It breeds in large open areas in the Palearctic and is migratory, wintering in Africa, the Indian subcontinent and parts of the Arabian Peninsula. Its common name commemorates the British naturalist George Montagu.

children.name: Falco macrourus
children.description: Falco macrourus — a species in the genus Circus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila
children.description: Aquila — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Aquila antiqua
children.description: Aquila antiqua — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila bivia
children.description: Aquila bivia — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila braccata
children.description: Aquila braccata — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila bullockensis
children.description: {{Speciesbox | fossil_range = Middle Miocene | image = | image_caption = | genus = Aquila | species = bullockensis | authority = Gaff & Boles, 2010 }}.

children.name: Aquila corroyi

children.name: Aquila danana
children.description: Aquila danana — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila delphinensis
children.description: Aquila delphinensis — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila fucosa
children.description: Aquila fucosa — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila hypogaea
children.description: Aquila hypogaea — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila lydekkeri
children.description: Aquila lydekkeri — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila pennatoides
children.description: Aquila pennatoides — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Aquila pezopora
children.description: Aquila pezopora — a species in the genus Aquila, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Harpia
children.description: Harpia — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Buteo
children.description: Buteo — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Buteo ales
children.description: Buteo ales — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo antecursor
children.description: Buteo antecursor — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo archeri
children.description: Archer's buzzard is a subspecies of the augur buzzard that is endemic to Somalia. The bird's common name and Latin binomial commemorate the British explorer and colonial official Sir Geoffrey Francis Archer.

children.name: Buteo auguralis
children.description: The red-necked buzzard, also known as the African red-tailed buzzard, is a species of buzzard in the family Accipitridae which is found in western and northern central Africa.

children.name: Buteo brachypterus
children.description: The Madagascar buzzard is a bird of prey which is endemic to Madagascar. It is a species from the widespread genus Buteo in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Buteo burmanicus
children.description: Buteo burmanicus may refer to two different species of bird in the genus Buteo:.

children.name: Buteo buteo
children.description: The common buzzard is a medium-sized bird of prey with a large range. It is a member of the genus Buteo in the family Accipitridae. The species lives in most of Europe and extends its breeding range across much of the Palearctic as far as northwestern China, far western Siberia and northwestern Mongolia.

children.name: Buteo conterminus
children.description: Buteo conterminus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo contortus
children.description: Buteo contortus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo dananus
children.description: Buteo dananus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo dondasi
children.description: Buteo dondasi — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo fluviaticus
children.description: Buteo fluviaticus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo galapagoensis
children.description: The Galápagos hawk is a large hawk endemic to most of the Galápagos Islands.

children.name: Buteo grangeri
children.description: Buteo grangeri — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo hemilasius
children.description: The upland buzzard is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. The largest species of the Buteo genus, this buzzard lives in mountainous grassy and rocky areas in areas of Central Asia, northern South Asia and East Asia from Kazakhstan to Korea. The upland buzzard is migratory but typically covers a short distance apparently to avoid snow cover that may hamper prey capture.

children.name: Buteo hoffstetteri
children.description: Buteo hoffstetteri — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo lagopus
children.description: The rough-legged buzzard (Europe) or rough-legged hawk is a medium-large bird of prey. It is found in arctic and subarctic regions of North America, Europe, and Asia during the breeding season, and migrates south for the winter. Historically, it was also known as "rough-legged falcon" in such works as John James Audubon's The Birds of America.

children.name: Buteo lineatus
children.description: The red-shouldered hawk is a medium-sized buteo. Its breeding range spans eastern North America and along the coast of California and northern to northeastern-central Mexico. It is a permanent resident throughout most of its range, though northern birds do migrate, mostly to central Mexico.

children.name: Buteo nitidus
children.description: The gray-lined hawk is a smallish raptor found in open country and forest edges. It is sometimes placed in the genus Asturina as Asturina nitida. The species has been split by the American Ornithological Society from the gray hawk.

children.name: Buteo pusillus
children.description: Buteo pusillus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo regalis
children.description: The ferruginous hawk is a large bird of prey and belongs to the broad-winged buteo hawks. An old colloquial name is ferrugineous rough-leg, due to its similarity to the closely related rough-legged hawk.

children.name: Buteo rufinus
children.description: The long-legged buzzard is a bird of prey found widely in several parts of Eurasia and in North Africa. This species ranges from Southeastern Europe down to East Africa to the northern part of the Indian subcontinent. The long-legged buzzard is a member of the genus Buteo, being one of the larger species therein.

children.name: Buteo sanfelipensis
children.description: Buteo sanfelipensis — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo socotraensis
children.description: The Socotra buzzard is a medium to large bird of prey that is sometimes considered a subspecies of the widespread common buzzard. As its name implies, it is native to the island of Socotra, Yemen. It is listed as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List, as its small population is believed to be decreasing.

children.name: Buteo spassovi
children.description: Buteo spassovi is an extinct species of Buteo that lived during the Late Miocene.

children.name: Buteo swainsoni
children.description: Swainson's hawk is a large bird species in the Accipitriformes order. This species was named after William Swainson, a British naturalist. It is colloquially known as the grasshopper hawk or locust hawk, as it is very fond of Acrididae and will voraciously eat these insects whenever they are available.

children.name: Buteo typhoius
children.description: Buteo typhoius — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteo ventralis
children.description: The rufous-tailed hawk is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Calohierax quadratus
children.description: Calohierax quadratus — a species in the genus Buteo, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Chondrohierax
children.description: Chondrohierax — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Morphnus
children.description: Morphnus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Morphnus woodwardi
children.description: Morphnus woodwardi — a species in the genus Morphnus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Accipiter
children.description: Accipiter — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Accipiter badius
children.description: The shikra is a small bird of prey in the family Accipitridae found widely distributed in Asia and Africa where it is also called the little banded goshawk. The African forms may represent a separate species but have usually been considered as subspecies of the shikra. The shikra is very similar in appearance, as well as behavior, at least to some degree, to other species including the Chinese sparrowhawk, Eurasian goshawk and Eurasian sparrowhawk.

children.name: Accipiter cooperii
children.description: Cooper's hawk is a medium-sized hawk native to the North American continent and found from southern Canada to Mexico. This species was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter.

children.name: Accipiter gentilis
children.description: The Eurasian goshawk is a species of medium-large bird of prey in the family Accipitridae, a family which also includes other extant diurnal raptors, such as eagles, buzzards and harriers. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter. It is a widespread species that inhabits many of the temperate parts of Eurasia.

children.name: Accipiter melanoleucus
children.description: The black sparrowhawk, sometimes known as the black goshawk or great sparrowhawk, is a bird of prey belonging to the family Accipitridae. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter. It occurs mainly in forest and non-desert areas south of the Sahara, particularly where there are large trees suitable for nesting; favored habitat includes suburban and human-altered landscapes.

children.name: Accipiter nisus
children.description: The Eurasian sparrowhawk, also known as the northern sparrowhawk or simply the sparrowhawk, is a small bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. Adult male Eurasian sparrowhawks have bluish grey upperparts and orange-barred underparts; females and juveniles are brown above with brown barring below. The female is up to 25% larger than the male – one of the greatest size differences between the sexes in any bird species.

children.name: Accipiter striatus
children.description: The sharp-shinned hawk or northern sharp-shinned hawk, colloquially often nicknamed sharpie, is a small hawk, with males being the smallest hawks in the United States and Canada, but with the species averaging larger than some Neotropical species, such as the tiny hawk. The taxonomy is far from resolved, with some authorities considering the southern taxa to represent three separate species, white-breasted hawk, plain-breasted hawk, and rufous-thighed hawk. The American Ornithological Society and some other checklists keep all four species conspecific.

children.name: Accipiter tachiro
children.description: The African goshawk is an African species of bird of prey in the genus Aerospiza. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter. The African goshawk was previously sometimes split with the red-chested goshawk of west Africa treated as a separate species.

children.name: Lophoictinia
children.description: Lophoictinia — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Urotriorchis
children.description: Urotriorchis — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Gypaetus
children.description: Gypaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Gypaetus grandis
children.description: Gypaetus grandis — a species in the genus Gypaetus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteogallus
children.description: Buteogallus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Buteogallus borrasi
children.description: Buteogallus borrasi is a species of giant buteonine hawk which went extinct in the early Holocene.

children.name: Buteogallus irpus
children.description: Buteogallus irpus — a species in the genus Buteogallus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteogallus meriodionalis
children.description: Buteogallus meriodionalis — a species in the genus Buteogallus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Buteogallus royi
children.description: Buteogallus royi — a species in the genus Buteogallus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Geranoaetus fragilis
children.description: Geranoaetus fragilis — a species in the genus Buteogallus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Urubitinga milleri
children.description: Urubitinga milleri — a species in the genus Buteogallus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Polyboroides
children.description: Polyboroides — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Circaetus
children.description: Circaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Geranoaetus
children.description: Geranoaetus — a genus of hawks, eagles & kites in the family Accipitridae.

children.name: Geranoaetus ales
children.description: Geranoaetus ales — a species in the genus Geranoaetus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Geranoaetus conterminus
children.description: Geranoaetus conterminus — a species in the genus Geranoaetus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Milvus aegyptius
children.description: The yellow-billed kite is the Afrotropical counterpart of the black kite, of which it is most often considered a subspecies. However, DNA studies suggest that the yellow-billed kite differs significantly from black kites in the Eurasian clade, and should be considered as a separate, allopatric species.

children.name: Milvus cenchris
children.description: Milvus cenchris — a species in the genus Milvus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Milvus incertus
children.description: Milvus incertus — a species in the genus Milvus, family Accipitridae. Also known as hawks, eagles & kites.

children.name: Milvus migrans
children.description: The black kite or fire kite is a medium-sized bird of prey in the family Accipitridae, which also includes many other diurnal raptors. It is thought to be the world's most abundant species of Accipitridae, although some populations have experienced dramatic declines or fluctuations. Current global population estimates run up to 6.7 million individuals.

children.name: Milvus milvus
children.description: The red kite is a bird of prey in the family Accipitridae, which also includes many other diurnal raptors such as eagles, buzzards, and harriers. The species currently breeds only in Europe, though it formerly also bred in West Asia and Northwest Africa. Historically, it was only resident in the milder parts of its range in Western Europe and Northwestern Africa, whereas all or most red kites in Northern mainland Europe wintered to the south and west, some also reaching western Asia, but an increasing number of northern birds now remain in that region year-round.
